requirement_acceptance_summary: A mentor record carries at least one availability window after registration.
requirement_description: When a mentor registers, capture their weekly windows.

requirement_acceptance_summary: ACCEPTED WHEN: (1) Storage - every first-class entity table persists a created timestamp at insert and, for mutable types, a last-edited timestamp updated on every write; immutable types persist created only. Any first-class browsable type currently missing a created_at or updated_at column gains it via Alembic migration. (2) UI - every entity panel that lists records shows a formatted Created column, and every detail view shows formatted Created and Last Updated rows, using the shared ui/widgets/datetime_format.format_timestamp helper (local time, YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM). (3) Immutable types render their last-edited value as an em dash, not a fabricated or duplicated timestamp. (4) Raw unformatted ISO timestamp columns currently shown by the Sessions / Workstreams / Conversations panels are replaced with the formatted helper. (5) Tests cover the helper-rendered columns/rows per panel, mirroring tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_planning_items_timestamps.py. VERIFIED BY: the V2 UI test suite remaining green and a manual pass confirming each panel shows the two timestamps.
requirement_description: Every first-class object type in the V2 system (the governance and methodology entities that each have a browsable desktop UI panel: charter, status, decision, session, risk, planning_item, topic, workstream, conversation, reference_book, work_ticket, close_out_payload, deposit_event, commit, domain, entity, field, requirement, persona, process, manual_config, test_spec, crm_candidate, engagement, reference) must persist a created datetime and a last-edited datetime, and must surface both to the user in the desktop UI. Today the storage side is largely complete (24 of 24 first-class entities carry created_at and 23 carry updated_at) but only the Planning Items panel displays them (PI-107, commit f690bde). This requirement makes timestamp visibility universal across every entity panel and closes the small number of schema gaps. Scope (SES-120 decision): first-class UI entities only; subsidiary catalog reference tables and internal edge/log tables are out of scope. Immutable / append-only types (deposit_event, reference, topic, charter, status, reference_book_version) are created-only by design and are not forced to gain an updated_at column.
requirement_name: Universal created and last-edited timestamps, stored and visible in the UI
